title: Kubernetes and Helm deployment
description: K8s-native deployment artifacts: Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Ingress manifests plus a Helm chart for parameterising image tag, replicas, and resource limits.

title: Implement /readyz readiness probe
description: Add a /readyz route that returns 200 only after all configured APIs have finished loading their specs. Returns 503 during startup. Complements the existing /healthz liveness probe. Needs the startup failure isolation work done first so the gateway tracks which APIs loaded successfully.

title: Write GitLab CI pipeline
description: Create .gitlab-ci.yml with stages: lint (ruff/mypy), test (pytest unit tests, pytest integration tests), build (docker build), smoke (docker smoke test). Unit and integration test jobs run in parallel. Build depends on lint+test passing. Smoke test depends on build. Use python:3.13-slim for test jobs and docker:latest for build/smoke. Cache uv/pip downloads between runs.

title: Pin dependencies in pyproject.toml
description: Add fastmcp, httpx, uvicorn, pyyaml, starlette (and any transitive pins) to pyproject.toml dependencies. Currently the deps list is empty. Use uv to resolve and lock versions. This is a prerequisite for the Dockerfile build.

title: Add spec caching and SIGHUP reload
description: Cache fetched OpenAPI specs to disk (configurable cache_dir, default .spec-cache/). On startup, use cached spec if fresh (configurable TTL). On SIGHUP, reload all specs from source and rebuild FastMCP instances without restarting the process.

title: Add config validation with clear errors
description: Validate config.yaml at startup: required fields (spec, base_url), valid auth values (none, bearer_passthrough), valid tls_verify type. Raise ValueError with a human-readable message naming the offending key before any network calls are made.

title: Add admin endpoint
description: Add GET /admin/apis endpoint returning a JSON array of loaded API info: name, spec source URL or path, auth type, tool count, last_loaded_at timestamp. Useful for ops visibility without restarting the gateway.

title: Implement bearer_static auth
description: Add auth: bearer_static support. The gateway reads the token from an env var named <UPPERCASE_API_NAME>_API_TOKEN (e.g. FOO_API_TOKEN for api named foo). Inject it as a static Authorization: Bearer header on all upstream requests. Document in README and config reference. K8s Secret -> env var pattern for injecting tokens.

title: Implement graceful shutdown
description: Ensure in-flight requests complete before the process exits on SIGTERM. uvicorn supports this natively via --timeout-graceful-shutdown. Wire it through the CLI argument parsing and document the default value. Add a lifespan context manager if needed for clean client teardown.

title: Add route filtering config option
description: Add an optional filter key per API in config.yaml. Supports include/exclude lists of OpenAPI operation tags, paths (glob), or HTTP methods. Apply filters when building the RouteMap list passed to FastMCP.from_openapi(). Document the filter syntax in config.yaml example and README.

title: Core gateway hardening
description: P0 items needed for a usable, reliable deployment: pinned dependencies, local spec file support, per-API startup failure isolation, and config validation with clear error messages.

title: Add structured JSON logging
description: Replace print() calls with structured JSON log lines using Python's logging module. Each upstream API call should emit a log record with fields: api, method, path, duration_ms, status. Startup and error events should also be structured. Use log_level from CLI/env.

title: Add petstore OpenAPI fixture
description: Create tests/fixtures/petstore.json with a minimal but realistic Petstore OpenAPI 3.x spec (a few endpoints across two tags). This fixture is used by both unit and integration tests to avoid any network calls.

title: Write K8s manifests
description: Create k8s/ directory with: deployment.yaml (1 replica, liveness on /healthz, readiness on /readyz, configmap volume mount at /config), service.yaml (ClusterIP on port 8000), configmap.yaml (config.yaml key with example apis block), ingress.yaml (optional, TLS termination). Use app=oapi2mcp labels throughout.

title: Testing
description: Full test pyramid: unit tests for config parsing and auth middleware, integration tests using a mock upstream with the petstore fixture, container smoke test, and K8s smoke test in a kind cluster.

title: Write Helm chart
description: Create helm/ directory with Chart.yaml, values.yaml (image.tag, replicaCount, resources.requests/limits, ingress.enabled), and templates/ that wrap the K8s manifests. values.yaml must be the single place to tune the deployment. Depends on K8s manifests existing as reference.

title: Core gateway hardening
description: P0 items needed for a usable deployment: pinned deps, local spec file support, startup failure isolation, config validation.

title: Production hardening
description: P1 operational quality items: graceful SIGTERM shutdown, structured JSON logging, /readyz readiness probe, and per-API route filtering by tag/path/method.

title: Containerization
description: Build and verify the Docker container image. Covers multi-stage Dockerfile using uv for deps and a Docker smoke test to confirm the image boots and /healthz returns 200.

title: Write integration tests
description: Create tests/test_integration.py. Use respx or a Starlette TestClient with a mock upstream. Steps: spin up the gateway pointed at the petstore fixture, make MCP tool calls via HTTP, assert correct Authorization header forwarding for bearer_passthrough, assert no Authorization header for auth=none, assert /healthz and /readyz return correct status.

title: Isolate per-API startup failures
description: In build_gateway(), wrap each load_api() call in a try/except. On failure, log a warning with the API name and error, skip that API, and continue loading the rest. Gateway should start with partial API set rather than crashing entirely.

title: P2 enhancements
description: Nice-to-have features: bearer_static auth (token from env var), spec caching with SIGHUP reload, admin endpoint showing loaded APIs and tool counts, and Prometheus metrics endpoint.

title: Add Prometheus metrics endpoint
description: Expose GET /metrics in Prometheus text format. Metrics: oapi2mcp_requests_total{api,method,status} counter, oapi2mcp_request_duration_seconds{api} histogram. Use prometheus-client library. Add to pyproject.toml deps and document the scrape endpoint in README.

title: Docker container smoke test
description: Add a Makefile target (make smoke-test or similar) that: docker builds the image, docker runs it with a minimal config.yaml mounted, waits for /healthz to return 200, then stops the container. Should work in CI without a K8s cluster. Depends on Dockerfile.

title: K8s smoke test
description: Document and script a smoke test for the K8s manifests using a local kind cluster: kind create cluster, kubectl apply -f k8s/, kubectl port-forward, curl /healthz. Can be a Makefile target or a shell script in scripts/. Depends on K8s manifests.

title: Write unit tests with mock objects
description: Create tests/test_unit.py. Use unittest.mock or pytest-mock to test in isolation without running a real server. Cover: (1) Config parsing — valid config, missing required fields, invalid auth value, tls_verify type. (2) BearerPassthroughMiddleware — token is extracted and set in contextvar, non-bearer auth falls through, non-http scope is passed through unchanged. (3) TokenPropagatingClient — Authorization header is injected when contextvar is set, omitted when empty. (4) load_api() — local spec path is read from disk (mock open), HTTP spec URL is fetched (mock httpx), auth=none uses plain AsyncClient, auth=bearer_passthrough wraps app in middleware. All external I/O must be mocked.

title: Support local spec files in config
description: Detect when spec value is a relative path or file:// URI and read the OpenAPI spec from disk instead of making an HTTP GET. Needed for local dev and for bundling specs in the container image.

title: Write README.md
description: Write a comprehensive README.md covering: (1) What oapi2mcp is and the problem it solves. (2) Architecture diagram (ASCII, same as PLAN.md). (3) Quick start — docker run one-liner with a minimal config.yaml. (4) Full config.yaml reference — all fields, types, defaults, auth values table, tls_verify. (5) MCP client configuration examples (JSON snippet for Claude Desktop / .mcp.json). (6) K8s deployment guide — apply manifests, ConfigMap pattern for config, secrets pattern for bearer_static tokens. (7) Helm chart usage. (8) Health and readiness endpoints. (9) Structured log format. (10) Development setup — uv install, run locally, run tests.

title: Documentation
description: README and supporting docs so anyone unfamiliar with the service can understand how it works and deploy it without reading the code. Covers architecture overview, config reference, Docker quickstart, K8s deployment guide, and MCP client configuration examples.

title: Write multi-stage Dockerfile
description: Implement the multi-stage Dockerfile: builder stage installs deps with uv from pyproject.toml, runtime stage copies installed site-packages and gateway.py. ENV defaults HOST=0.0.0.0 PORT=8000 LOG_LEVEL=info. ENTRYPOINT python gateway.py, CMD --config /config/config.yaml. Depends on pyproject.toml having pinned deps.
